• Objects:
L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
n ≥ 0
• ei ∈ Elt (element abstract data type)

ﬁrst element of the list;

• emptyList()
• input: empty
• output: L = () (empty list)
• insert()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
k ∈ Nat,
e ∈ Elt
• output:
• L = (· · · , ek−1, e, ek , · · · ), if 0 ≤ k ≤ n
• error otherwise
L = (a, b, c, d, e, f , g )

• insert(L, 0, x) ⇒ L = (x, a, b, c, d, e, f , g )
Obs. the index of elements a, · · · , g is incremented by 1.
• insert(L, 2, x) ⇒ L = (a, b, x, c, d, e, f , g )
• insert(L, 7, x) ⇒ L = (a, b, c, d, e, f , g , x)
• insert(L, 10, x) ⇒ error
• insert(L, −7, x) ⇒ error

• delete()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
k ∈ Nat
• output:
• L = (· · · , ek−1, ek+1, · · · ), if 0 ≤ k ≤ n − 1
• error otherwise
Examples:
L = (a, b, c, d, e, f , g )

• delete(L, 2) ⇒ L = (a, b, d, e, f , g )
Obs. the index of elements d, · · · , g is decremented by 1.
• delete(L, 10) ⇒ error
• delete(L, −7) ⇒ error

• theKth()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
k ∈ Nat
• output:
• ek , if 0 ≤ k ≤ n − 1
• error otherwise
Examples:
L = (a, b, c, d, e, f , g )

• theKth(L, 0) ⇒ a
• theKth(L, 2) ⇒ c
• theKth(L, 6) ⇒ g
• theKth(L, 20) ⇒ error
• theKth(L, −2) ⇒ error

• deleteALLe()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
e ∈ Elt
• output:
• The L list where all occurrences of e have been deleted.
Examples:
L = (a, b, c, a, b, c, a)

• deleteALLe(L, a) ⇒ (b, c, b, c)
• deleteALLe(L, c) ⇒ (a, b, a, b, a)
• deleteALLe(L, d) ⇒ (a, b, c, a, b, c, a)

• iterate()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1), a procedure / function visit()
• output:
• The L list where all the elements have been processed by visit()
Examples:

• iterate(L, twoTimes()) ⇒ (2, 4, 6, 2, 4, 6)
• iterate(L, increment()) ⇒ (2, 3, 4, 2, 3, 4)

• pos()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
e ∈ Elt,
• output:
• the position of the ﬁrst occurence of e in L or
• −1 if e does not appear in L.
L = (a, b, c, a, b, c, d)
Examples:

• pos(L, a) ⇒ 0
• pos(L, c) ⇒ 2
• pos(L, d) ⇒ 6
• pos(L, x) ⇒ −1

• length()
• input:
• L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
• output:
• n – the length of L list.
Example:
• length(L) ⇒ 7
L = (a, b, c, a, b, c, d)

• Object representation: L = (e0, · · · , en−1)
L.tab
Elt[Max]
e0
. . .
en−1
Max-1
L.last
int
• L is a structure
• L.tab – an array ﬁeld that stores the list elements;
• L.last – a numeric ﬁeld that stores the last element position.

• insert()
• shift right by one position the elements of indices k, k + 1, . . . ;
• insert e on the position k;
• exceptions:
• k < 0,
k > L.last + 1
(n)
• L.last = Max − 1.

procedure insert(L, k, e)
begin
if (k < 0 or k > L.last + 1) then

throw “error-wrong position”
if (L.last >= Max − 1) then
throw “error-not enough memory”
for j ← L.last downto k do
L.tab[j + 1] ← L.tab[j]
L.tab[k] ← e
L.last ← L.last + 1
end
• Running time: O(n).

• iterate()
procedure iterate(L, visit())
begin
for i ← 0 to L.last do
visit(L.tab[i])
end
• If visit() processes one element in O(1), then iterate() processes the
list in O(n) (n the list length).

• Object representation: L = (e0, · · · , en−1)

• L is a structure with two ﬁelds
• L.ﬁrst – pointer to the ﬁrst element;
• L.last – pointer to the last element.
• a nod * p (stored at the address in p) has two ﬁelds:
• p− > elt(= ei ) – stores the node information;
• p− > succ – stores the address of the next node.

procedure insert(L, k, e)
begin
if (k < 0) then
if (k == 0 or L.ﬁrst == NULL) then
if (j < k − 1) then

j ← j + 1
throw “error-wrong position”
q− > succ ← p− > succ;
if (p == L.last) then
L.last ← q
p− > succ ← q
end

• Polygonal line.
• Point: structure with two ﬁelds x and y ;
• list building
procedure buildList(L)
begin
L ← emptyList()
/* read n */
for i ← 0 to n − 1 do
/* read p.x, p.y */
insert(L, 0, p)
end
• Obs. Running time depends on the implementation.

• Muliply by 2 one point coordinates:
procedure times2Point(p)
begin
p.x ← p.x ∗ 2
p.y ← p.y ∗ 2
end
• Muliply by 2 the coordinates of a polygonal line:
procedure times2Line(p)
begin
iterate(L, times2Point())
end

• translate point:
procedure trPoint(p, dx, dy )
begin
p.x ← p.x + dx
p.y ← p.y + dy
end
• translate polygonal line:
procedure trLine(L, dx, dy )
begin
iterate(L, trPoint())
end

• Objects:
L = (e0, · · · , en−1),
n ≥ 0,
ei ∈ Elt,
e0 ≤ e1 ≤ · · · ≤ en−1
• Operations:
• emptyList()
• input: empty
• output: L = () (empty list)
• insert()
• input: L = (e0, . . . , en−1),
• output: L = (· · · , ek−1, e, ek , · · · ), if ek−1 ≤ e ≤ ek
e ∈ Elt
(e−1 = −∞, en = +∞)

• delete()
• input: L = (e0, . . . , en−1),
• output: L = (· · · , ek−1, ek+1, · · · ), if e = ek
e ∈ Elt
error otherwise
• theKth()
• iterate()
• pos()

function pos(L, e)
begin
q ← L.last
p ← 0;
m ← (p + q)/2
while (L.tab[m]! = e and p < q) do
if (e < L.tab[m]) then
q ← m–1
else
p ← m + 1
m ← (p + q)/2
if (L.tab[m] == e) then
return m
else
return −1
end

• Array implementation: O(log2 n);
• Linked list implementation: O(n) (linear search).

• Objects:
Lists where the element age is known:
LIFO lists (Last-In-First-Out).
• Operations:
• emptyStack()
• input: empty
• output: S = () (empty stack)
• isEmpty()
• input: S ∈ Stack
• output:
– true if S is empty;
– false if S is not empty.

• Operations:
• push()
• input: S ∈ Stack,
e ∈ Elt
• output: S where e has been added as the last element
(the newest).
• pop()
• input: S ∈ Stack
• output:
– S where the last introduced element has been deleted
(the newest);
– error if S is empty.
• top()
• input: S ∈ Stack
• output:
– the last introduced in S element (the newest);
– error if S is empty.

ADT Stack
ADT LLin
push(S, e) = insert(S, 0, e)
pop(S, e) = delete(S, 0)
= theKth(S, 0)
top(S)
or
ADT Stack
ADT LLin
push(S, e) = insert(S, length(S), e)
pop(S, e) = delete(S, length(S) − 1)
= theKth(S, length(S) − 1)
top(S)
ADT — Abstract Data Type

• Object representation
S.tab

• implementation
procedure push(S, e)
begin
if S.last == Max − 1 then
throw “error”
else
S.last ← S.last + 1
S.tab[last] ← e
end

• Object representation
S
•
en−1 •

•

• Implementation
• push()
procedure push(S, e)
begin
new(q)
q− > elt ← e
q− > succ ← S
S ← q
end
• pop()
procedure pop(S)
begin
if S == NULL then
throw “error”
q ← S
S ← S− > succ
delete(q) end

• Direct applications
• waiting lists / threads;
• shared resources access.
Example: printers.
• Indirect applications
• Auxiliary data structure in certain algorithms.

• Objects:
Lists where the age elements is known:
FIFO lists (First-In-First-Out).
• Operations:
• emptyQueue()
• input: empty
• output: C = () (empty queue)
• isEmpty()
• input: C ∈ Queue
• output:
– true if C is empty;
– false if C is not empty.

• Operations:
• insert()
• input: C ∈ Queue,
e ∈ Elt
• output: C where e has been added as the last element
(newest).
• delete()
• input: C ∈ Queue
• output:
– C whehre the ﬁrst added element has been deleted
(oldest);
– error if C is empty.
• read()
• input: C ∈ Queue
• output:
– the ﬁrst introduced element in C (oldest);
– error if C is empty.

ADT Queue
ADT LLin
insert(C , e) = insert(C , length(C ), e)
delete(C )
read(S)
= delete(C , 0)
= theKth(C , 0)

• Implementation
• insert()
procedure insert(C , e)
begin
if (C .last + 1)%Max == C .ﬁrst then
throw “error”
else
C .last ← (C .last + 1)%Max
C .tab[last] ← e
end

• Object representation

C.last

• Implementation
• insert()
procedure insert(C , e)
begin
new(q)
q− > elt ← e
q− > succ ← NULL
if C .last == NULL then
C .ﬁrst ← q
C .last ← q
else
C .last− > succ ← q
C .last ← q
end

procedure convInﬁx2Postﬁx(inﬁx, postﬁx)
/* inﬁx s, i postﬁx sunt cozi*/
begin
S ← emptyStack()
while (not isEmpty (inﬁx)) do
x ← read(inﬁx);
if (operand(x) then
insert(postﬁx, x)
delete(inﬁx)
else
if (x ==(cid:48))(cid:48)) then

pop(S)

priorit(top(S)) >= priorit(x)) do

Postﬁx expression evaluation
Example: a b c d + ∗ + e+
postf.tab

↓
S (stack)
a + b ∗ (c + d) + e

function valPostﬁx(postﬁx)
begin
S ← emptyStack()
while (not isEmpty (postﬁx)) do
delete(inﬁx)
x ← read(postﬁx);
if (operand(x) then
push(S, x)
else
right ← top(S);
left ← top(S);
val ← left op(x) right
push(S, val)
pop(S)
pop(S)
val = top(S);
return val
pop(S)
end
